title: Riot Platforms Reports Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results
text: Riot Platforms reported record Q2 2025 financial results with net income of $219.5 million, total revenue of $153.0 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $495.3 million. Total revenue reached $153.0 million, up 119% from $70.0 million in Q2 2024. Riot delivered impressive Bitcoin production metrics, mining 1,426 BTC (averaging 15.7 BTC daily) while maintaining a Bitcoin treasury of 19,273 BTC valued at approximately $2.1 billion. Cost to mine per Bitcoin increased from $43,808 in Q1 2025 to $48,992 in Q2 2025, primarily due to 9% increase in global network hash rate and property tax reassessment at Corsicana facility. The company announced hiring of Jonathan Gibbs as chief data center officer, secured a $200 million bitcoin-collateralized facility with Coinbase, and continued land acquisitions around its Corsicana, Texas campus. Riot brought in $219.5 million in net income over Q2 2025, driven mainly by a $470.8 million unrealized revaluation of its bitcoin treasury.
